summary: Iteration 21 — Odometer toggle bug fix verification. Tested backend endpoint and full frontend flow for Field Tracking > Odometer tab. All 12 backend tests and 3 frontend toggle tests passed. Bug fix confirmed: projection `{_id:0, employee_id:1, odometer_required:1}` + `if emp is None` check correctly handles employees without the odometer_required field set.

test_results.backend_odometer_employees_list: PASS (5/5) — GET /api/tracker/odometer/employees returns 200, list of employees with correct fields, booleans, no exited employees, 401/403 for unauth
test_results.backend_toggle_returns_200: PASS — POST /api/tracker/odometer/toggle/{id} returns 200 with odometer_required bool
test_results.backend_toggle_false_to_true: PASS — toggling an employee with odometer_required=False correctly flips to True and persists (verified via GET)
test_results.backend_toggle_true_to_false: PASS — full round-trip: False→True→False, both states persisted
test_results.backend_bug_fix_missing_field: PASS — core regression test: employee without odometer_required field returns 200 (not 404). Fix confirmed.
test_results.frontend_tab_loads: PASS — Odometer tab loads 46 employees with toggle switches visible
test_results.frontend_toggle_enable: PASS — clicking grey toggle turns it green (bg-[#12855a] class added), API call successful
test_results.frontend_toggle_disable: PASS — clicking green toggle turns it grey (bg-slate-300 class returned), API call successful
test_results.frontend_toggle_persistence: PASS — navigating away and back re-fetches list and preserves enabled state

critical_code_review_comments: Bug fix is correct: tracker.py line 757 projection now includes `employee_id: 1` alongside `odometer_required: 1`, and line 759 uses `if emp is None` instead of `if not emp`. This correctly handles the case where find_one returns `{}` (dict with no extra fields) vs None (employee not found).
The /odometer/employees endpoint also correctly uses `bool(e.get('odometer_required'))` to handle missing field gracefully.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed data. Toggle states were reverted to original during test cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Odometer toggle bug fix fully verified (iteration 21). Fix was a MongoDB projection issue in toggle_odometer endpoint: projection {_id:0, odometer_required:1} returned {} for employees without the field (falsy), causing false 404. Fixed by adding employee_id to projection and using `if emp is None`. Both backend (12/12 tests) and frontend (3/3 toggle flows) are working. DB state clean after tests.

odometer_toggle_404_false_positive.root_cause: MongoDB projection `{_id:0, odometer_required:1}` returns `{}` (empty dict) when employee exists but doesn't have `odometer_required` field set. Empty dict is falsy in Python. The original code `if not emp: raise 404` was incorrectly treating existing employees (without the field) as not found.
odometer_toggle_404_false_positive.fix_applied: 1. Added `employee_id: 1` to projection so returned doc is never empty for a real employee. 2. Changed guard to `if emp is None` which correctly distinguishes 'employee not found' (None) from 'employee found but field missing' ({}).
odometer_toggle_404_false_positive.test_verification: test_toggle_bug_fix_employee_without_odometer_field passes — employee with odometer_required=False (field not set in DB) now toggles successfully to True without 404.
